morta_nuts2
  • Plot Class
  • View page source

Plot Class

plot_class

This module gathers all visualisation classes and utilities used for demographic and actuarial analysis at the regional (NUTS) level.

It provides five main classes:

  • RegionalCurvePlotter — compares mortality curves across models and regions as a grid of subplots.

  • MapPlotter — generates NUTS-level choropleth maps from demographic indicators (life expectancy, model errors, etc.).

  • RegionalStats — computes descriptive statistics (mean, standard deviation, average rank) on a projected regional indicator.

  • DispersionPlotter — visualises the evolution of inter-regional dispersion (standard deviation and range) over the projection horizon.

  • AnnuityBoxPlotter — draws regional boxplots of annuity prices, with optional overlay of national or benchmark series.

The module also exposes the dataclass ExtraSeries and two internal helper functions for boxplot styling (_resolve_style(), _boxplot_kwargs()).

Dependencies: geopandas, matplotlib, numpy, pandas, pathlib, dataclasses.

NUTS shapefile: SHAPEF_PATH is resolved automatically by walking up the directory tree from the current working directory until a NUTS_files folder is found.

Quick-start example:

# Compare mortality curves for the year 2040
plotter = RegionalCurvePlotter(
    x_values=ages,
    curves_dict={"Model A": arr_a, "Model B": arr_b},
    tv=tv_vector,
    regions=region_list,
)
plotter.plot(year_to_plot=2040)
plotter.save("output/curves_2040.png")

# Choropleth map for 2030
mp = MapPlotter(regions, data_3d, tv_future, "FR", "Life expectancy e0")
mp.plot_single_year(year=2030)
mp.save("output/map_2030.png")
morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.SHAPEF_PATH = WindowsPath('C:/Users/Idrissa Belem/Documents/GitHub/morta_nuts2/NUTS_files/NUTS_RG_01M_2024_3035.shp')

Path to the NUTS shapefile used for choropleth maps.

class morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.ExtraSeries(data: ndarray, label: str, position: str = 'last', style: dict = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Describes an additional data series to overlay on top of the regional boxplots in AnnuityBoxPlotter.

Parameters:

  • data (numpy.ndarray) – Numpy array of shape (nb_xe, 1, nb_simul) containing the simulations for this series.

  • label (str) – Display name used in the legend and x-axis tick labels.

  • position (str) – Insertion position of the boxplot relative to regions. "first" places it before the regional boxes, "last" after.

  • style (dict) – Optional visual override dictionary. Valid keys: facecolor, edgecolor, median, linewidth. If empty or None, a color from the default palette is applied.

data: ndarray
label: str
position: str = 'last'
style: dict
class morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.RegionalCurvePlotter(x_values, curves_dict, tv, regions, n_cols=3, yscale='log', xlabel='Age', ylabel='Mortality rate', title_prefix='Comparison')[source]

Bases: object

Plots multiple model curves for each region as a grid of subplots, producing one figure per group of n_cols regions.

Typically used to compare mortality rates or other demographic indicators from different models for a given projection year.

Parameters:

  • x_values (array-like) – Common x-axis values shared by all curves (e.g. age groups).

  • curves_dict (dict) – Dictionary {label: 3D_array} where each array has shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • tv (numpy.ndarray) – 1D array of available projection years.

  • regions (list) – List of region names.

  • n_cols (int) – Number of columns in the subplot grid (default: 3).

  • yscale (str) – Y-axis scale, "log" or "linear" (default: "log").

  • xlabel (str) – X-axis label (default: "Age").

  • ylabel (str) – Y-axis label (default: "Mortality rate").

  • title_prefix (str) – Prefix string used in the figure title (default: "Comparison").

plot(year_to_plot)[source]

Generate and display one figure per group of n_cols regions for the requested projection year.

With n_cols=3 (default) and 22 regions, this produces 8 figures of 3 panels each. Figures are also stored in self._figs for a subsequent call to save().

Parameters:

year_to_plot (int) – Year to display. Must exist in self.tv.

Raises:

ValueError – If year_to_plot is not found in self.tv.

save(path, dpi=150, **kwargs)[source]

Save all figures produced by plot() to disk.

path is used as a base: the suffix _part1, _part2, … is inserted before the file extension.

Example: "output/curves_2040.png" produces "output/curves_2040_part1.png" and "output/curves_2040_part2.png".

Parameters:

  • path (str or pathlib.Path) – Base destination file path.

  • dpi (int) – Resolution in dots per inch (default: 150).

  • kwargs – Additional arguments forwarded to matplotlib.savefig().

Raises:

RuntimeError – If plot() has not been called yet.

class morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.MapPlotter(regions, data, tv_future, country_code, indicator_name, shapef_path=WindowsPath('C:/Users/Idrissa Belem/Documents/GitHub/morta_nuts2/NUTS_files/NUTS_RG_01M_2024_3035.shp'), cmap='Blues', nuts_level=2, age=0)[source]

Bases: object

Renders choropleth maps of a demographic indicator at the NUTS regional level from an Eurostat shapefile.

Several display modes are available:

  • plot_single_year() — one map for a given year.

  • plot_compare_years() — side-by-side maps for multiple years sharing a common colour scale.

  • plot_static() — map without a time dimension (aggregated data or mean errors).

  • plot_compare_models() — side-by-side comparison of two models using a shared diverging colour scale.

Parameters:

  • regions (array-like) – NUTS region codes (e.g. ["FR10", "FR21", ...]).

  • data (numpy.ndarray) – Data array of shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions) or (horizon, nb_regions). If 3-D, the age slice age is extracted at initialisation.

  • tv_future (numpy.ndarray) – 1D array of projection years.

  • country_code (str) – ISO country code used to filter the shapefile (e.g. "FR").

  • indicator_name (str) – Indicator label displayed in the colorbar and title.

  • shapef_path (pathlib.Path) – Path to the NUTS shapefile (default: SHAPEF_PATH).

  • cmap (str) – Matplotlib colormap name (default: "Blues").

  • nuts_level (int) – NUTS level to display (default: 2).

  • age (int) – Age index to slice when data is 3-D (default: 0).

plot_single_year(year=2023)[source]

Draw a single choropleth map for the specified projection year.

The figure is stored in self._fig for a subsequent call to save().

Parameters:

year (int) – Projection year to display (default: 2023).

plot_compare_years(years=None)[source]

Draw side-by-side choropleth maps for several years using a shared colour scale to allow direct comparison.

The figure is stored in self._fig for a subsequent call to save().

Parameters:

years (list[int]) – List of years to compare (default: [2023, 2050]).

plot_static(static_data, title_suffix='')[source]

Draw a choropleth map from data that has no time dimension (e.g. mean errors or aggregated indicators).

Accepts shapes (nb_regions,) or (nb_ages, nb_regions). In the latter case, the age index defined at initialisation is used.

Parameters:

  • static_data (numpy.ndarray) – Data to map, of shape (nb_regions,) or (nb_ages, nb_regions).

  • title_suffix (str) – Optional subtitle displayed on the map (e.g. "Mean error").

Raises:

ValueError – If static_data is neither 1-D nor 2-D.

save(path, dpi=150, **kwargs)[source]

Save the last rendered map to disk.

Parameters:

  • path (str or pathlib.Path) – Destination file path (e.g. "output/map_2030.png").

  • dpi (int) – Resolution in dots per inch (default: 150).

  • kwargs – Additional arguments forwarded to matplotlib.savefig().

Raises:

RuntimeError – If no plot method has been called yet.

plot_compare_models(other_data, model_labels=('Model A', 'Model B'), static=False, year=None, cmap_diverging='RdYlGn_r')[source]

Display the values (errors or any indicator) of two models side by side on a map, with a shared diverging colour scale so differences are immediately visible.

The normalisation is symmetric around zero for signed data (e.g. bias) and min/max for strictly positive data (e.g. RMSE, MAE).

The figure is stored in self._fig for a subsequent call to save().

Parameters:

  • other_data (numpy.ndarray) – Data for the second model, same shape as self.data ((horizon, nb_regions)) or (nb_regions,) when static=True.

  • model_labels (tuple[str, str]) – Titles for the left and right panels (default: ("Model A", "Model B")).

  • static (bool) – If True, both datasets are treated as time-independent (default: False).

  • year (int or None) – Projection year to display when static=False. If None, the first year in tv_future is used.

  • cmap_diverging (str) – Colormap applied to both panels simultaneously. A diverging colormap (e.g. "RdYlGn_r", "coolwarm") is recommended for error maps (default: "RdYlGn_r").

Note

The figure produced can be saved with save().

Example:

mp = MapPlotter(regions, errors_model_a, tv, "FR", "RMSE")
mp.plot_compare_models(errors_model_b, model_labels=("LLP", "Lee-Carter"))
mp.save("output/compare_models.png")
class morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.RegionalStats(ex, regions, tv_future, age=0)[source]

Bases: object

Computes descriptive statistics on a projected regional indicator over the full projection horizon.

Produces three tables: the complete (horizon × nb_regions) matrix, per-year aggregates, and per-region aggregates sorted by descending mean.

Parameters:

  • ex (numpy.ndarray) – Data array of shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions).

  • regions (array-like) – Region codes or names.

  • tv_future (numpy.ndarray) – 1D array of projection years.

  • age (int) – Age index to extract (0 = e0, 65 = e65, …) (default: 0).

compute()[source]

Run all aggregations and return a dictionary of pandas DataFrames.

Per-year statistics include mean, standard deviation, minimum, maximum, range, and coefficient of variation. Per-region statistics additionally include the average rank (rank 1 = highest value each year).

Returns:

Dictionary containing three DataFrames:

  • "full" — complete (horizon × nb_regions) matrix.

  • "by_year" — statistics aggregated per projection year.

  • "by_region" — statistics aggregated per region, sorted by descending mean.

Return type:

dict[str, pandas.DataFrame]

class morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.DispersionPlotter(ex, regions, tv_future, indicator_name='Life expectancy', ages=None)[source]

Bases: object

Visualises the evolution of inter-regional dispersion of an indicator over the full projection horizon.

Two metrics are plotted side by side: the inter-regional standard deviation and the range (max − min), making it easy to assess whether regions are converging or diverging over time.

Parameters:

  • ex (numpy.ndarray) – Data array of shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions).

  • regions (array-like) – Region codes or names (kept for consistency with other classes; not directly used in the plot).

  • tv_future (numpy.ndarray) – 1D array of projection years.

  • indicator_name (str) – Indicator label displayed in the figure title (default: "Life expectancy").

  • ages (list[int]) – List of age indices to compare (default: [0, 65]).

plot()[source]

Generate and display two side-by-side panels:

  • Left panel — inter-regional standard deviation over time.

  • Right panel — inter-regional range (max − min) over time.

One curve per age index in self.ages is drawn in each panel. The figure is stored in self._fig.

save(path, dpi=150, **kwargs)[source]

Save the last rendered dispersion figure to disk.

Parameters:

  • path (str or pathlib.Path) – Destination file path.

  • dpi (int) – Resolution in dots per inch (default: 150).

  • kwargs – Additional arguments forwarded to matplotlib.savefig().

Raises:

RuntimeError – If plot() has not been called yet.

class morta_nuts2.graph.plot_class.AnnuityBoxPlotter(price_regional: ndarray, region_names: list, extra_series: list = None, xe_idx: int = 0, duration: int = 20, age: int = 60, figsize: tuple = (18, 7), show: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Draws regional boxplots of annuity prices, with optional overlay of extra series (national price, benchmark, etc.) placed before or after the regional boxes.

Extra series are defined via ExtraSeries instances and can be positioned before ("first") or after ("last") the regional block. Thin vertical separators visually delimit the groups.

Parameters:

  • price_regional (numpy.ndarray) – Regional price array of shape (nb_xe, nb_reg, nb_simul).

  • region_names (list[str]) – List of region label strings.

  • extra_series (list[ExtraSeries] or None) – List of ExtraSeries objects to overlay. Pass [] or None to display regional data only.

  • xe_idx (int) – Index along the age dimension of price_regional (default: 0).

  • duration (int) – Annuity duration in years, used in the chart title (default: 20).

  • age (int) – Policyholder age, used in the chart title (default: 60).

  • figsize (tuple) – Figure size in inches (default: (18, 7)).

  • show (bool) – If True, calls plt.show() after rendering (default: True).

plot(ax=None)[source]

Render and display the boxplot chart.

Display order: "first" extra series → regional boxes → "last" extra series. Thin vertical separators delimit the blocks when extra series are present.

The figure is stored in self._fig for a subsequent call to save().

Parameters:

ax (matplotlib.axes.Axes or None) – Existing matplotlib axes to draw into. If None, a new figure is created.

Returns:

The figure and axes used.

Return type:

tuple(matplotlib.figure.Figure, matplotlib.axes.Axes)

save(path, dpi=150, **kwargs)[source]

Save the last rendered boxplot figure to disk.

Parameters:

  • path (str or pathlib.Path) – Destination file path.

  • dpi (int) – Resolution in dots per inch (default: 150).

  • kwargs – Additional arguments forwarded to matplotlib.savefig().

Raises:

RuntimeError – If plot() has not been called yet.
